title: Beginner 20: Reasoning Data Orientation
goal: Build the vocabulary for post-training reasoning data before diving into recipes.

entry.title: Datasheets for datasets
entry.one_line_summary: Introduces dataset datasheets: a structured documentation template for provenance, composition, collection process, recommended uses, and limitations.
entry.why_it_matters: It gives reasoning-data releases a minimum disclosure standard before anyone reuses prompts, traces, labels, rewards, or benchmark items.
entry.audit_focus: check links, lineage, verifier, split, and contamination

entries.title: Data statements for natural language processing

entry.title: Data statements for natural language processing
entry.one_line_summary: Proposes data statements for NLP datasets, foregrounding language, speaker/community provenance, annotation context, and intended deployment boundaries.
entry.why_it_matters: Reasoning-data users need this lens when a corpus mixes web text, synthetic questions, human annotations, or domain-specific tasks whose population assumptions affect generalization.
entry.audit_focus: check links, lineage, verifier, split, and contamination

entries.title: Training language models to follow instructions with human feedback

entry.title: Training language models to follow instructions with human feedback
entry.one_line_summary: InstructGPT establishes the demonstration, preference-comparison, reward-model, and PPO pipeline that many later post-training recipes inherit.
entry.why_it_matters: It is the alignment-data baseline for separating supervised demonstrations, pairwise preferences, learned rewards, and policy optimization in later reasoning models.
entry.audit_focus: check links, lineage, verifier, split, and contamination

entries.title: Chain-of-thought prompting elicits reasoning in large language models

entry.title: Chain-of-thought prompting elicits reasoning in large language models
entry.one_line_summary: Shows that few-shot natural-language rationales can elicit multi-step reasoning behavior from sufficiently large language models.
entry.why_it_matters: It is the conceptual bridge from answer-only prompts to trace-shaped reasoning examples, which later become SFT, distillation, filtering, and verifier targets.
entry.audit_focus: check links, lineage, verifier, split, and contamination

entries.title: Training verifiers to solve math word problems

entry.title: Training verifiers to solve math word problems
entry.one_line_summary: Introduces GSM8K and trains verifier models to rank model-generated math solutions by likely correctness.
entry.why_it_matters: It anchors answer-level math reasoning data as a pair of problem, solution, and verifier-selection signal, anticipating RLVR and reward-model workflows.
entry.audit_focus: check links, lineage, verifier, split, and contamination

entry.title: STaR: Bootstrapping reasoning with reasoning
entry.one_line_summary: STaR iteratively generates rationales, keeps examples whose final answers are correct, and fine-tunes on the accepted reasoning traces.
entry.why_it_matters: It is a compact recipe for self-improving reasoning data: model traces become training data only after answer-based filtering.
entry.audit_focus: check links, lineage, verifier, split, and contamination

entry.title: Self-Instruct: Aligning language models with self-generated instructions
entry.one_line_summary: Self-Instruct bootstraps instruction-following data by having a model generate instructions, inputs, and outputs, then filtering low-quality or duplicate examples.
entry.why_it_matters: It is the canonical self-generated instruction-data recipe that later reasoning datasets adapt for prompt sourcing and synthetic expansion.
entry.audit_focus: check links, lineage, verifier, split, and contamination

entry.title: Direct preference optimization: Your language model is secretly a reward model
entry.one_line_summary: DPO trains a policy directly from preference pairs by turning the reward-model objective into a supervised contrastive optimization problem.
entry.why_it_matters: It shows that pairwise preference data can shape post-training behavior without deploying a separate learned reward model during optimization.
entry.audit_focus: check links, lineage, verifier, split, and contamination

entry.title: Let's Verify Step by Step
entry.one_line_summary: Provides step-level human labels for mathematical reasoning traces and trains process reward models to identify correct intermediate reasoning.
entry.why_it_matters: It is the process-supervision anchor for moving from answer-level math rewards to step-level feedback in reasoning-model training and evaluation.
entry.data_object: step-level labels and final answers; process: step, label, solution trace; offline math reasoning traces
entry.feedback_verifier: process reward model trained from step labels
entry.audit_focus: check links, lineage, verifier, split, and contamination

entry.title: GSM8K: Grade School Math 8K
entry.one_line_summary: Canonical grade-school math benchmark with natural-language word problems, worked solutions, and final numeric answers.
entry.why_it_matters: It remains a compact sanity check for answer-verifiable reasoning data, verifier reranking, SFT, and RLVR-style math training.
entry.data_object: natural-language solution with final numeric answer; process: question, solution, final answer; offline math benchmark
entry.feedback_verifier: answer extraction and arithmetic correctness checks
entry.audit_focus: answer extraction errors, contamination through benchmark reuse

entry.title: Measuring mathematical problem solving with the MATH dataset
entry.one_line_summary: Introduces MATH, a competition-style math benchmark with challenging problems, subject categories, and step-by-step solutions.
entry.why_it_matters: MATH became a central answer-verifiable surface for evaluating and training advanced mathematical reasoning beyond grade-school word problems.
entry.audit_focus: check links, lineage, verifier, split, and contamination

entry.title: HumanEval: Hand-Written Evaluation Set
entry.one_line_summary: HumanEval provides hand-written Python programming problems with unit tests for executable code-generation evaluation.
entry.why_it_matters: It made unit-test execution a standard verifier for code reasoning, pass@k reporting, and later code-data filtering recipes.
entry.data_object: Python function completion; process: prompt, canonical solution, unit tests; Python execution harness
entry.audit_focus: public benchmark contamination, unit-test coverage gaps

entry.title: SWE-bench: Can language models resolve real-world GitHub issues?
entry.one_line_summary: SWE-bench turns real GitHub issues into repository-level repair tasks evaluated by applying patches and running tests.
entry.why_it_matters: It is the agent/environment anchor where the reasoning-data object includes repository state, issue text, actions, patches, and test-backed outcomes.
entry.audit_focus: check links, lineage, verifier, split, and contamination

entry.title: RewardBench: Evaluating Reward Models for Language Modeling
entry.one_line_summary: RewardBench evaluates reward models on prompt/chosen/rejected trios spanning chat, reasoning, and safety, including structured preference failures.
entry.why_it_matters: It helps readers test whether a reward signal generalizes beyond helpfulness style into subtle factual, reasoning, refusal, and safety preferences.
entry.data_object: pairwise or scalar reward decisions; process: prompt, chosen/rejected response, reward model score; offline preference benchmark
entry.audit_focus: check links, lineage, verifier, split, and contamination

entry.title: HealthBench
entry.one_line_summary: HealthBench evaluates healthcare conversations with physician-written, conversation-specific rubrics across safety, accuracy, communication, and domain contexts.
entry.why_it_matters: It is a high-stakes example of judgment-required reasoning data where rubric design matters more than exact-match scoring.
entry.data_object: response with rubric/judge evaluation; process: prompt, response, rubric dimension; offline health evaluation benchmark
entry.audit_focus: check links, lineage, verifier, split, and contamination

entry.title: LiveBench: A challenging, contamination-free benchmark for large language models
entry.one_line_summary: LiveBench is a frequently updated, contamination-limited benchmark that uses recent sources and objective scoring across math, code, reasoning, language, instruction following, and data analysis.
entry.why_it_matters: It gives reasoning-data readers a benchmark-refresh pattern for separating real progress from memorized or stale evaluation items.
entry.audit_focus: check links, lineage, verifier, split, and contamination

entry.title: OpenThoughts: Data recipes for reasoning models
entry.one_line_summary: OpenThoughts studies open data recipes for reasoning models through large public reasoning datasets and many controlled pipeline experiments.
entry.why_it_matters: It is one of the most useful open references for how question sourcing, filtering, teacher traces, and answer generation choices change downstream reasoning performance.
entry.data_object: reasoning traces and final answers; process: question, reasoning trace, answer; offline reasoning corpus
entry.feedback_verifier: filters, benchmark feedback, and recipe ablations
entry.audit_focus: check links, lineage, verifier, split, and contamination

entry.title: DeepSeek-R1
entry.one_line_summary: DeepSeek-R1 reports a reasoning-model post-training recipe centered on reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards, cold-start data, and distillation.
entry.why_it_matters: It is a frontier reference for public RLVR discussion, showing how verifiable tasks, reward design, and distillation shape reasoning behavior.
entry.audit_focus: check links, lineage, verifier, split, and contamination

entry.title: s1: Simple Test-Time Scaling
entry.one_line_summary: s1 curates a small s1K reasoning dataset and studies budget forcing as a simple way to scale test-time reasoning compute.
entry.why_it_matters: It is a useful counterpoint to massive-data recipes: careful small-set curation plus inference-budget control can materially change reasoning performance.
entry.audit_focus: check links, lineage, verifier, split, and contamination

entries.title: A Sober Look at Progress in Language Model Reasoning

entry.title: A Sober Look at Progress in Language Model Reasoning: Pitfalls and Paths to Reproducibility
entry.one_line_summary: Audits reasoning-model progress claims by showing that benchmark results can be highly sensitive to decoding, seeds, prompt format, and environment details.
entry.why_it_matters: It is an audit anchor for this atlas: reasoning-data claims need reproducible evaluation settings, not just headline benchmark gains.
entry.audit_focus: check links, lineage, verifier, split, and contamination

entry.title: Leaky Thoughts
entry.one_line_summary: Leaky Thoughts shows that reasoning traces from personal-agent settings can expose sensitive user data through prompt injection or accidental leakage.
entry.why_it_matters: It turns chain-of-thought and test-time compute into a privacy audit problem: more internal reasoning can increase utility while enlarging the attack surface.
entry.data_object: internal reasoning trace, final answer, and leakage/extraction outcome.; process: sensitive field, prompt-injection condition, reasoning length or budget, output leakage indicator.; personal-agent evaluation setting with hidden or internal reasoning traces.
entry.audit_focus: Hiding thoughts from users does not make them safe., Trace logging can create a new privacy dataset., Utility improvements from more reasoning may worsen leakage risk.

entry.title: Subliminal Learning
entry.one_line_summary: Subliminal Learning shows that teacher models can transmit behavioral traits through semantically unrelated generated data, even after visible trait references are filtered.
entry.why_it_matters: It is a data-lineage warning for reasoning distillation: synthetic traces may carry hidden model traits that are invisible to content filters.
entry.data_object: generated data plus downstream behavioral evaluation of the student.; process: teacher identity, student base model, visible filtering policy, hidden trait evaluation.; distillation and synthetic-data training pipeline.
entry.audit_focus: Data may look safe while carrying hidden traits., Lineage effects can be invisible from sample inspection., Distillation chains can propagate behavior across model generations.

entry.title: AbstentionBench
entry.one_line_summary: AbstentionBench evaluates whether LLMs know when not to answer across unknown, underspecified, false-premise, subjective, and stale-information questions.
entry.why_it_matters: It is a direct audit surface for reasoning models: stronger reasoning can still fail if the model confidently answers unanswerable questions instead of abstaining.
entry.data_object: model response, abstention decision, and correctness/abstention judgment.; process: scenario type, source dataset, answerability label, judge/validation metadata.; offline benchmark with model-evaluation harness.
entry.feedback_verifier: human-validated judges and benchmark labels for abstention scenarios.
entry.audit_focus: A model can game abstention by refusing too often., Benchmark labels around subjectivity and underspecification can be ambiguous., Prompt tuning may improve benchmark score without improving epistemic reasoning.

entry.title: Introducing SWE-bench Verified
entry.one_line_summary: SWE-bench Verified is a human-filtered 500-instance subset of SWE-bench designed to reduce ambiguous, unsolvable, or incorrectly tested software-engineering tasks.
entry.why_it_matters: It is now a central coding-agent evaluation surface because the data object contains issue text, repository state, proposed patch, and test-backed success criteria.
entry.data_object: patch diff applied to a repository plus test execution results.; process: repository, issue, base commit, patch, FAIL TO PASS tests, PASS TO PASS tests, human validation notes.; Dockerized repository checkout and unit-test harness.
entry.feedback_verifier: post-patch unit tests plus human filtering of task validity.
entry.audit_focus: Leaderboard scores can depend strongly on scaffold design., Tests may not cover all acceptable patches., Public benchmark tasks can become training targets over time.

entry.title: Constitutional AI: Harmlessness from AI feedback
entry.one_line_summary: Constitutional AI trains harmless behavior from AI-generated critiques, revisions, and AI preference feedback guided by a written constitution.
entry.why_it_matters: It is a core recipe for replacing part of human feedback with principle-guided model feedback, making critiques and preference pairs first-class post-training data.
entry.data_object: original answer, self-critique, revised answer, preference pair, reward-model score.; process: principle used, critique, revision, comparison, preference label.; offline SL and RLHF/RLAIF alignment pipeline.
entry.feedback_verifier: AI preference model trained from comparisons guided by constitutional principles.
entry.audit_focus: AI feedback can encode model bias at scale., Principles may be underspecified or culturally narrow., A model can become safe-looking but evasive if helpfulness is not audited.

entry.title: Step-DPO: Step-wise Preference Optimization for Long-chain Reasoning of LLMs
entry.one_line_summary: Step-wise preference optimization method for long-chain reasoning traces.
entry.why_it_matters: It helps readers see how preference optimization becomes a process-level data problem when the chosen/rejected object is an intermediate continuation rather than a whole answer.
entry.data_object: step-wise preference pairs; process: reasoning step, preferred continuation, rejected continuation; offline long-chain reasoning traces
entry.audit_focus: local step preference may not align with final correctness, preference construction can hide teacher or scorer bias, long-chain traces can overfit style instead of reasoning validity

entry.title: UltraFeedback: Boosting language models with high-quality feedback
entry.one_line_summary: UltraFeedback releases large-scale AI feedback with fine-grained ratings and critiques over diverse instruction-response pairs.
entry.why_it_matters: It is a widely reused preference/reward data source, but its value depends on auditing prompt sources, judge model behavior, rubric dimensions, and corrected labels.
entry.data_object: instruction, candidate responses, fine-grained ratings, textual critiques, and derived preference pairs.; process: source dataset, model identity, response, rating dimension, critique text, corrected overall score.; offline feedback generation and reward-model training pipeline.
entry.feedback_verifier: AI-generated scalar and textual feedback over response quality dimensions.
entry.audit_focus: AI feedback can encode judge-model bias., A corrected dataset version can change reward-model behavior., Fine-grained scores may not translate cleanly into pairwise preferences.

entry.title: Let's Verify Step by Step
entry.one_line_summary: Provides step-level human labels for mathematical reasoning traces and trains process reward models to identify correct intermediate reasoning.
entry.why_it_matters: It is the process-supervision anchor for moving from answer-level math rewards to step-level feedback in reasoning-model training and evaluation.
entry.data_object: step-level labels and final answers; process: step, label, solution trace; offline math reasoning traces
entry.feedback_verifier: process reward model trained from step labels

entry.title: Math-Shepherd
entry.one_line_summary: Rollout-value supervision method that assigns process rewards to intermediate math reasoning steps.
entry.why_it_matters: It is the clearest bridge between final-answer verifiers and step-level PRM data: the label is not a human judgment but a rollout-derived estimate of whether a partial step can still reach the right answer.
entry.data_object: step-level rollout-value labels; process: reasoning step, rollout result, process reward score; offline math reasoning traces
entry.audit_focus: rollout policy strength can leak into labels, step rewards may favor locally plausible continuations, generated solutions can inherit base-model shortcuts

entry.title: Subliminal Learning
entry.one_line_summary: Subliminal Learning shows that teacher models can transmit behavioral traits through semantically unrelated generated data, even after visible trait references are filtered.
entry.why_it_matters: It is a data-lineage warning for reasoning distillation: synthetic traces may carry hidden model traits that are invisible to content filters.
entry.data_object: generated data plus downstream behavioral evaluation of the student.; process: teacher identity, student base model, visible filtering policy, hidden trait evaluation.; distillation and synthetic-data training pipeline.
entry.audit_focus: Data may look safe while carrying hidden traits., Lineage effects can be invisible from sample inspection., Distillation chains can propagate behavior across model generations.
